pattern: A monotonic stack of indices awaiting their next greater value lets one pass resolve every node's answer the moment a larger value appears.
hints: For each node you want the first later node with a strictly larger value, which is the classic next-greater-element question.
Read the list into an array, then keep a stack of positions whose answer is still unknown; when the current value beats the value at the stack's top, that top's answer is found.
While the current value exceeds the value at the index on top of the stack, pop and record it; then push the current index — leftover indices get answer zero.

edge_inputs.description: single node has no greater successor
ascending then nothing greater at the end
strictly increasing, each points to the next
duplicates need a strictly larger value

stress.description: long random list to exercise the monotonic stack
stress.note: wire form is the value array
